title: The Impact of Tourism on Local Cultures
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The (0) global expansion of tourism has brought both opportunities and challenges for local communities. When managed (17) responsibly, tourism can promote cross-cultural (18) understanding and create valuable economic (19) opportunities. Visitors often develop a deeper (20) appreciation for traditions and customs very different from their own.||However, mass tourism can also cause (21) significant damage to fragile environments and local ways of life. Some communities face the (22) commercialisation of their culture, turning (23) traditional practices into performances for tourists. To ensure (24) sustainability, governments and travel companies must work together to develop tourism models that respect and protect local heritage.

example.text: The (0) GLOBAL expansion of tourism has brought both opportunities and challenges for local communities. (GLOBE)

questions.explanation: The adverb 'responsibly' is formed from 'responsible' by replacing '-le' with '-ly'.
The noun 'understanding' is formed from 'understand' by adding the suffix '-ing'.
The plural noun 'opportunities' is formed from the root 'opportune' via 'opportunity' + '-ies'.
The noun 'appreciation' is formed from 'appreciate' by replacing '-ate' with '-ation'.
The adjective 'significant' is formed from 'signify' by replacing '-ify' with '-icant'.
The noun 'commercialisation' is formed from 'commerce' via 'commercial' + '-isation'.
The adjective 'traditional' is formed from 'tradition' by adding the suffix '-al'.
The noun 'sustainability' is formed from 'sustain' by adding the suffix '-ability'.
